FaqSection.subtitle: FAQ
FaqSection.title: Your Questions, Answered
FaqSection.q1: Is health insurance mandatory for my residence permit?
FaqSection.a1: Yes, in Germany, valid health insurance is a requirement for all residents and for obtaining any long-term visa or residence permit. We help you choose the one that's right for your situation.
FaqSection.q2: Why should I switch from Public (GKV) to Private (PKV)?
FaqSection.a2: Private insurance often offers better services, faster specialist access, and can be cheaper if you are a young high-earner or freelancer. However, it's a long-term decision that needs careful planning.
FaqSection.q3: What is the difference between 'Netto' and 'Brutto' for pensions?
FaqSection.a3: Brutto is your gross salary before taxes and social contributions. Netto is what's in your bank account. Your pension will be a percentage of your last Netto income, hence the 'pension gap'.
FaqSection.q4: How much does a personal consultation cost?
FaqSection.a4: My initial consultation is 100% free and without any obligation. I'm compensated by the insurance providers, so you get expert advice at no extra cost.
FaqSection.q5: Can I manage my insurance in English?
FaqSection.a5: Yes! At Next Gen Capital, we offer multilingual support, and I personally advice in 4 languages. Most documentation is also available in English for expats.

Timeline.subtitle: Your Success Journey
Timeline.title: From Call to Covered
Timeline.desc: A seamless 5-step journey from first contact to full protection — typically done in under 7 days.
Timeline.step1Desc: 30-minute conversation in your language. No pressure, no jargon. We map out your situation and identify the gaps.
Timeline.step2Desc: Within 48 hours, I build your personalized insurance architecture — comparing products, costs, and tax advantages.
Timeline.step3Desc: A simple, visual proposal in your language. Everything explained. No surprises in the fine print — ever.
Timeline.step4Desc: Fully digital, bilingual forms. We handle the paperwork. You're covered and activated in days, not weeks.
Timeline.step5Desc: Life changes. I'm still here. Annual reviews, job changes, family updates — your coverage evolves with you.
